response: This code computes the length of the longest common subsequence (LCS) between two strings `a` and `b` using dynamic programming. It fills a 2D table `dp` where each cell `dp[i][j]` stores the LCS length for the prefixes `a[:i]` and `b[:j]`, updating it by either adding 1 when the current characters match or taking the maximum of the values from the cell above or to the left when they don’t.
